description: Near Miss Juice — fix multiplier relevance, add visual/audio feedback escalation, redesign combo HUD, and close the feedback loop with game over stats.

userStories.title: Combo color palette utility + global score multiplier
userStories.description: As a player, I want my combo multiplier to affect ALL score sources (not just near miss base points) so that maintaining a combo streak has a real, noticeable impact on my total score.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a `getComboColor(level)` function that returns: x1='#FF8800' (orange), x2='#FFD700' (gold), x3='#00FFDD' (cyan), x4+='#FF44FF' (magenta)
Distance score (line ~4612: `effSpeed * dt * SCORE_PER_PX`) is multiplied by `comboMultiplier` when combo >= 2
Overtake bonus (line ~3552: `OVERTAKE_BONUS`) is multiplied by `comboMultiplier` when combo >= 2
Coin pickup score (line ~3802: `COIN_POINTS`) is multiplied by `comboMultiplier` when combo >= 2
Survivor bonus (line ~4617: `SURVIVOR_BONUS`) is NOT multiplied — it stays fixed
Near miss base points continue multiplied as before (line ~2784-2785)

Add tracking variables at module scope: `let nearMissCount = 0`, `let bestCombo = 0`, `let nearMissBonusTotal = 0` — incremented in `triggerNearMiss()`. `bestCombo` updated via `bestCombo = Math.max(bestCombo, comboMultiplier)`. All three reset in `resetGame()`
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: getComboColor() will be reused by all subsequent stories for consistent color theming. Tracking vars are added here so data accumulates from the start.

userStories.title: Float text shows points + multiplier at player position
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see the actual points gained and the current multiplier in the near miss floating text, spawned at my car's position (not behind me where the vehicle already passed).
acceptanceCriteria: Modify `spawnFloatText()` to accept an optional `scale` parameter (default 1.0) that multiplies the rendered font size
Modify `triggerNearMiss()`: the float text now shows points and multiplier — format: '+50' at x1, 'x2 +100!' at x2, 'x3 +150!' at x3, etc.
The float text color uses `getComboColor(comboMultiplier)` instead of hardcoded '#FF8800'
The float text scale increases with combo: `1.0 + (comboMultiplier - 1) * 0.12` (capped at 1.5)
The float text spawns at the player's center position (`playerCenterX, player.y + PLAYER_HEIGHT * 0.3`) — NOT at the vehicle's position
Existing float texts for coins (+1), fuel (+FUEL), nitro (+NITRO), shield (+SHIELD) are unchanged
The float text render function respects the scale parameter when setting font size
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The near miss detection fires AFTER the vehicle passes the player (v.y > player.y + PLAYER_HEIGHT), so the vehicle is already below/behind. Spawning text at the player position ensures the player sees it.

userStories.title: Redesign combo HUD with timer bar and animations
userStories.description: As a player, I want a prominent, animated combo indicator with a visible countdown bar so I know my combo level and feel urgency to maintain it.
acceptanceCriteria: Rewrite `renderComboHUD(ctx)` — displays 'x{N} COMBO' in large bold font (28-32px) at center-top (y ~45, below score)
Text color uses `getComboColor(comboMultiplier)`
Below the text, render a horizontal timer bar (width ~120px, height ~6px) showing `comboResetTimer / COMBO_RESET_TIME` as fill ratio
Timer bar color transitions: green (>66%), yellow (33-66%), red (<33%) based on remaining ratio
Pop/bounce animation on each new near miss: scale jumps to 1.35 then eases back to 1.0 over COMBO_SCALE_DURATION (0.2s) — use existing `comboScaleTimer`
When combo expires (comboResetTimer reaches 0), the HUD shakes briefly (2-3px horizontal oscillation for 0.15s) then fades out over 0.3s
At milestone levels (x3, x5, x8), the HUD briefly flashes white (0.1s) as a celebration pulse
HUD only renders when comboMultiplier >= 2 (unchanged from current behavior)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The existing renderComboHUD is small (~25 lines). The rewrite replaces it entirely. comboScaleTimer and comboResetTimer already exist and are ticked in the game loop.

userStories.title: Screen shake on near miss with priority system
userStories.description: As a player, I want to feel a short screen shake when I nail a near miss, with intensity that scales with my combo level.
acceptanceCriteria: In `triggerNearMiss()`, call `triggerShake()` with: duration = 0.12s, intensity = min(2 + comboMultiplier, 5)

Modify `triggerShake()` to only apply the new shake if its intensity is >= the remaining intensity of any current shake — this prevents near miss shake (2-5px) from overriding collision shake (10px) in progress
The remaining intensity of a shake is: `shake.intensity * (shake.time / shake.maxTime)`
Shake remains visual-only: applied via ctx.translate() offset, does not affect hitboxes or game logic
Collision shake (0.5s, 10px) continues to work unchanged and always overrides near miss shake
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: triggerShake() already exists at line ~2458. The collision handler calls triggerShake(0.5, 10). The change is: add a priority/intensity guard so weaker shakes don't replace stronger ones.

userStories.title: Near miss spark particles at player position
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see sparks fly from my car when I barely dodge a vehicle, reinforcing the feeling of speed and danger.
acceptanceCriteria: In `triggerNearMiss(v)`, emit particles from the player's side closest to the vehicle: if vehicle is to the right, sparks emit from player's right edge; if left, from left edge
Base particle count: 8 at x1, +3 per combo level, capped at 20
Each particle: random velocity (lateral away from vehicle +-80-150px/s, vertical -50 to +50px/s), lifespan 0.3-0.5s, fades out linearly
Particle color uses `getComboColor(comboMultiplier)` — all sparks from one near miss share the same combo color
Particles are added to the existing `particles[]` array and rendered/updated by the existing particle system
If the existing particle object shape needs new fields (e.g., color, alpha), add them without breaking existing explosion/collision particles
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The particles[] array already exists for collision sparks. Check the existing particle object shape before adding fields — maintain backward compatibility with existing particle types.

userStories.title: Combo edge glow on top/bottom borders
userStories.description: As a player, I want a subtle ambient glow on the screen edges while my combo is active, making the combo state feel immersive.
acceptanceCriteria: When `comboMultiplier >= 2`, render a radial gradient glow on the top and bottom edges of the canvas (NOT lateral edges — those are used by the danger flash)
Glow color uses `getComboColor(comboMultiplier)` with low alpha (~0.15-0.25)
Glow pulses subtly: alpha oscillates using sin() at ~1Hz (breathing effect)
Glow fades in over 0.3s when combo starts (comboMultiplier goes from 1 to 2) and fades out over 0.5s when combo expires
Glow is rendered AFTER the game world but BEFORE the HUD layer
Danger flash (red, lateral edges) and combo glow (combo color, top/bottom) can coexist without visual conflict — different colors and positions
Glow has negligible performance impact (two gradient draws per frame max)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The danger flash already exists (dangerFlashAlpha, red glow on lateral edges when traffic is near). The combo glow uses TOP/BOTTOM edges with the combo color palette to stay visually distinct.

userStories.title: Score punch animation on near miss points
userStories.description: As a player, I want the score display to visually react when I earn multiplied near miss points, connecting the near miss event to my score.
acceptanceCriteria: Add module-scope variables: `let scorePunchScale = 0` (0 = no punch, decays to 0), `let scorePunchColor = null`
In `triggerNearMiss()`, set `scorePunchScale = 0.15 + comboMultiplier * 0.05` (capped at 0.4) and `scorePunchColor = getComboColor(comboMultiplier)`
In the game loop update, decay `scorePunchScale` toward 0: `scorePunchScale = Math.max(0, scorePunchScale - dt * 3)`

In the score rendering code, when `scorePunchScale > 0`: apply ctx.scale(1 + scorePunchScale, 1 + scorePunchScale) around the score text, and temporarily use `scorePunchColor` as fill color (blending back to white as scale decays)
The punch is purely visual — it does not affect the score lerp or the actual score value
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The score rendering currently uses ctx.fillText with lerped displayScore. The punch adds a temporary scale transform and color flash around that same draw call.

userStories.title: Combo milestone SFX and combo lost SFX
userStories.description: As a player, I want escalating audio feedback at combo milestones and a deflation sound when my combo drops, reinforcing the risk/reward loop.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `AudioManager.playComboMilestone(level)` method: plays a bright ascending chord/ding that varies by level — higher pitch/more harmonics for x5 and x8 vs x3. Duration ~0.2s, volume 0.2
In `triggerNearMiss()`, call `playComboMilestone(comboMultiplier)` when comboMultiplier reaches exactly 3, 5, or 8 (check AFTER incrementing combo)
Add `AudioManager.playComboLost()` method: plays a short descending 'whoosh down' or deflation tone. Duration ~0.3s, volume 0.15
In the combo reset timer tick (line ~4657), when `comboResetTimer` reaches 0 AND `comboMultiplier` was > 1, call `playComboLost()` BEFORE resetting comboMultiplier to 1
Milestone SFX and combo lost SFX do not overlap with or drown out engine sound, crash SFX, or near miss whoosh
Verify playComboUp (existing) still works correctly — the new milestone SFX is additional, not a replacement
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: AudioManager uses Web Audio API with oscillators. playComboUp already exists and raises pitch with combo level. The new milestone SFX should be distinct — a celebratory ding vs the existing power chord. playComboLost needs a reference to comboMultiplier before it resets.

userStories.title: Camera zoom on high combo
userStories.description: As a player, I want to feel the intensity ramp up through a subtle zoom-in effect when I maintain a high combo streak.
acceptanceCriteria: Add module-scope variable: `let comboZoom = 1.0` (current zoom level, lerps toward target)
Target zoom: 1.0 at combo < 4, 1.02 at combo 4-5, 1.04 at combo 6+, capped at 1.05
In game loop update, lerp `comboZoom` toward target: `comboZoom += (target - comboZoom) * dt * 3` (smooth ease)
When combo is lost (comboMultiplier reset to 1), target becomes 1.0 — zoom eases back over ~0.5s due to lerp

In the play state render, apply zoom at the start of the frame: ctx.save(), ctx.translate(CANVAS_WIDTH/2, CANVAS_HEIGHT/2), ctx.scale(comboZoom, comboZoom), ctx.translate(-CANVAS_WIDTH/2, -CANVAS_HEIGHT/2) — then ctx.restore() at the end
The zoom is purely visual — hitboxes, spawn positions, and all game logic use unscaled coordinates
Zoom does NOT apply during explosion, game over, or title states — only during play state
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The zoom transform must wrap ALL rendering inside the play state's render function but be applied AFTER the ctx.save() that handles screen shake. Order: save → translate(shake) → translate+scale(zoom) → render world → restore.

userStories.title: Near miss stats on game over screen
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see my near miss performance (count, best combo, bonus points) on the game over screen so I understand how the mechanic impacted my score.
acceptanceCriteria: In `gameOverState.onEnter()`, capture tracking vars into gameOverState: `finalNearMissCount`, `finalBestCombo`, `finalNearMissBonusTotal`
In `gameOverState.render()`, if `finalNearMissCount > 0`, render a 'NEAR MISS' stats section below the existing stats (Time/Distance/Score) and above the ranking panel
Stats section shows: 'Near Misses: {count}  |  Best Combo: x{best}  |  Bonus: +{total}'
If `finalBestCombo >= 3`, render the stats in gold color (#FFD700) with a 'NICE!' label
If `finalNearMissCount === 0`, do not render the section at all (no empty/zero state)
Stats are read-only display — they do not affect score submission or ranking
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The tracking vars (nearMissCount, bestCombo, nearMissBonusTotal) are created in US-001. gameOverState already captures finalScore, finalDistance, finalTime, coinsCollected. The new stats follow the same capture pattern. May need to adjust RANK_Y or stats positioning to fit the new section.

userStories.title: First-time near miss discoverability hint
userStories.description: As a new player, I want to learn that near miss exists so I can use it intentionally to boost my score.
acceptanceCriteria: Add module-scope flag: `let nearMissHintShown = false` — reset in `resetGame()`
When the first traffic vehicle enters the screen (y > 0) and `nearMissHintShown` is false, spawn a float text at top-center of screen: 'Passe rente aos carros!' in white, with 3s duration
Set `nearMissHintShown = true` immediately after showing the hint
If the player triggers a near miss before the hint naturally expires, the hint disappears early (or is simply overtaken by the near miss float text due to the 8-item limit)
The hint does NOT pause the game, block input, or interfere with gameplay in any way
The hint appears only once per game session (resets on new game via resetGame, but not on page reload — no localStorage needed)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use the existing spawnFloatText system. The hint is a regular float text with longer duration (3s vs the normal FLOAT_TEXT_DURATION). May need to allow spawnFloatText to accept a custom duration parameter, or just set a longer timer directly.
